'''Dorlo''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Atravis sector/Legends|Atravis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and was controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Geography==
The surface of Dorlo was initially considered "beautiful" by its colonists, with warm days, fertile valleys with farms, [[Braal]] fields and rivers, and [[Water/Legends|snowy]] mountains. Its soil was rich in [[Ore/Legends|ore]] and supplied the Empire's production of weapons. During the SoroSuub occupation, many of these locations were dug into pits.<ref name="Agent">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

==History==
In ancient times, before the time of the Galactic Empire, an unknown ancient culture, that had developed architecture and [[Writing]] had left some traces on the planet. They had built long walls, covered with written characters, which were later buried under the ground.<ref name="Agent" />

Dorlo was settled by [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] about four generations before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Agent" />

[[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] sent [[reconnaissance droid]] to investigate its properties. Three local months later, Corporate [[Passenger liner/Legends|starliner]] arrived, bought cheaply the farms, and sent the settlers to a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] world at the borders with the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Some farmers attempted to protect their property by resisting; thirty-six were killed, and crudely made markers were put near a road intersection to mark that spot.<ref name="Agent" />

Freighters and shuttles landed ten thousand mining machines; located and operating by geolocation [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]], they produced fifty tons of ore each local day, which they loaded to the freighters.<ref name="Agent" />

While looking for resources, the SoroSuub [[Mining droid/Legends|mining droids]] uncovered a three-mile long wall, build ages ago by Dorlo's ancient inhabitants. Although the company wasn't much interested to preserve the ruin, they thought that the writing covering the effigy would provide more resources, and hired Professor [[Nij Por Ral]].<ref name="Agent" />

[[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]], who was after the [[Valley of the Jedi]] led some [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] allies to the planet in order to find the Professor and persuade him join them to his quest. The linguist reluctantly agreed to leave without the company's permission, knowing that his work would be sacrificed. With [[Duno Dree]], [[Cee Norley]] and [[Rolanda Gron]] they escaped Imperial forces on a [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmer]]; the [[Seven Dark Jedi]] were [[Mission to Dorlo|sent to capture them]] by [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and prevented their escape.<ref name="Agent" />

{{Planet-stub}}
